title: विष्णु कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विष्णु कवचम् ॥

॥ अथ श्री विष्णु कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्रीविष्णुकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहाविष्णुर्देवता।
श्रीमहाविष्णुप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

नारायणः परो देवस्तस्माज्जातश्चतुर्मुखः।
तस्माद्रुद्रोऽभवद्देवः स च सर्वज्ञतां गतः॥ १॥

ईशानः सर्वविद्यानामीश्वरः सर्वभूतानाम्।
ब्रह्माधिपतिर्ब्रह्मणोऽधिपतिर्ब्रह्मा शिवो मे अस्तु सदाशिवोम्॥ २॥

विष्णुर्मे पातु शिरसि केशवो मे ललाटकम्।
नारायणश्च नेत्रे मे माधवो नासिकां मम॥ ३॥

गोविन्दो मे मुखं पातु कण्ठं पातु जनार्दनः।
विष्णुः पातु भुजौ मे च करौ पातु सदाच्युतः॥ ४॥

हृदयं पातु गोविन्दो नाभिं पातु त्रिविक्रमः।
वामनो जठरं पातु मधुसूदन ऊरुकम्॥ ५॥

श्रीधरो जानुनी पातु जङ्घे पातु जनार्दनः।
पादौ पातु हृषीकेशः सर्वाङ्गं मे गदाधरः॥ ६॥

प्राच्यां पातु हृषीकेशो वासुदेवस्तथाग्नये।
दक्षिणे मे पातु हरिर्नैर्ऋत्यां माधवोऽवतु॥ ७॥

पश्चिमे पातु गोविन्दो वायव्यां पातु केशवः।
उत्तरे पातु विश्वात्मा ऐशान्यामच्युतोऽवतु॥ ८॥

ऊर्ध्वं पातु पद्मनाभो अधस्ताच्च जनार्दनः।
सर्वतो मां सदा पातु नारायणदुरासदः॥ ९॥

इदं विष्णोः कवचं दिव्यं मन्त्रपूतं सुदुर्लभम्।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते॥ १०॥

शत्रवो नास्तिकाश्चैव भयं तस्य न जायते।
सर्वरोगविनिर्मुक्तो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री विष्णु कवचम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vishnu Kavacham ||

|| Atha Shri Vishnu Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Vishnu-kavacha-stotra-mantrasya Brahma Rishih.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Maha-Vishnur-Devata.
Shri Maha-Vishnu-prityarthe jape viniyogah.

Narayanah paro devas-tasmaj-jatash-chaturmukhah.
Tasmad-rudro-abhavad-devah sa cha sarvajnatam gatah. || 1 ||

Ishanah sarva-vidyanam-ishvarah sarva-bhutanam.
Brahmadhipatir-brahmano-adhipatir-brahma shivo me astu sadashivom. || 2 ||

Vishnur-me patu shirasi Keshavo me lalaatakam.
Narayanash-cha netre me Madhavo nasikam mama. || 3 ||

Govindo me mukham patu kantham patu Janardanah.
Vishnuh patu bhujau me cha karau patu sada-Achyutah. || 4 ||

Hridayam patu Govindo nabhim patu Trivikramah.
Vamano jatharam patu Madhusudana urukam. || 5 ||

Shridharo januni patu janghe patu Janardanah.
Padau patu Hrishikeshah sarvangam me Gadadharah. || 6 ||

Prachyam patu Hrishikesho Vasudevas-tatha-agnaye.
Dakshine me patu Harir-nairityam Madhavo-avatu. || 7 ||

Pashchime patu Govindo vayavyam patu Keshavah.
Uttare patu Vishvatma aishanyam-Achyuto-avatu. || 8 ||

Urdhvam patu Padmanabho adhastaach-cha Janardanah.
Sarvato mam sada patu Narayana-durasadah. || 9 ||

Idam Vishnoh kavacham divyam mantra-putam sudurlabham.
Yah pathet pratar-utthaya sarva-papaih pramuchyate. || 10 ||

Shatravo nastikash-chaiva bhayam tasya na jayate.
Sarva-roga-vinimurkto Vishnu-lokam sa gachchhati. || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Vishnu Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
इस श्री विष्णु कवच स्तोत्र मन्त्र के ऋषि ब्रह्मा हैं, छन्द अनुष्टुप् है, देवता श्री महाविष्णु हैं।

नारायण परम देव हैं, उनसे चतुर्मुख ब्रह्मा उत्पन्न हुए, उनसे रुद्र देव उत्पन्न हुए जो सर्वज्ञता को प्राप्त हुए॥ १॥

विष्णु मेरे मस्तक की रक्षा करें, केशव मेरे ललाट की, नारायण मेरे नेत्रों की, माधव मेरी नासिका की रक्षा करें॥ ३॥

गोविन्द मेरे मुख की रक्षा करें, जनार्दन कण्ठ की, विष्णु भुजाओं की, अच्युत सदा मेरे हाथों की रक्षा करें॥ ४॥

गोविन्द हृदय की, त्रिविक्रम नाभि की, वामन पेट की, मधुसूदन जंघाओं की रक्षा करें॥ ५॥

श्रीधर घुटनों की, जनार्दन जंघाओं की, हृषीकेश पैरों की, गदाधर मेरे सम्पूर्ण अंगों की रक्षा करें॥ ६॥

पूर्व में हृषीकेश, अग्निकोण में वासुदेव, दक्षिण में हरि, नैर्ऋत्य में माधव, पश्चिम में गोविन्द, वायव्य में केशव, उत्तर में विश्वात्मा, ईशान में अच्युत रक्षा करें॥ ७-८॥

ऊपर पद्मनाभ, नीचे जनार्दन, सब ओर से सदा दुर्जय नारायण मेरी रक्षा करें। यह विष्णु का दिव्य कवच जो प्रातःकाल पढ़ता है वह सब पापों से मुक्त होता है, शत्रुओं से भय नहीं होता और विष्णुलोक को प्राप्त करता है॥ ९-११॥

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीविष्णुकवचस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहाविष्णुर्देवता। ॐ नमो नारायणाय बीजम्। विष्णुमाया शक्तिः। श्रीमहाविष्णुप्रीत्यर्थे विष्णुकवचजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Provides divine armor-like protection from all directions
Shields against enemies and negative forces
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins when recited in the morning
Invokes freedom from diseases and ailments
Invokes the blessing of fearlessness and courage
Leads the devotee to Vishnuloka after death
description: Vishnu Kavacham — विष्णु कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Vishnu Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to bestow fearlessness and courage and provide divine armor-like protection from all directions. It is also said to remove all sins when recited in the morning. For best results, devotees chant this on Thursday (Guruvar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Ekadashi. Comprising 11 verses, it can be completed in approximately 10 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vishnu Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vishnu Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Divine armor prayer to Lord Vishnu for protection from all directions.

seoFaqs.question: Where is the Vishnu Kavacham preserved in tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Kavacham circulates in several recensions in Pauranika literature. The most cited version is preserved in the Vishnu Purana and presents the standard limb-protection structure assigning each Vishnu name to a body part. Other respected versions appear in the Brahmanda Purana, the Garuda Purana within its protective-mantra section, and as part of the Sudarshana-related compilations in Pancharatra Agama texts. The shorter Vishnu Kavacham of around ten to twelve verses is the household-recitation standard, while the more elaborate Bhagavata Narayana Kavacham of Skanda 6 is reserved for serious protective sadhana. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia Vishnu Purana; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Vishnu Kavacham structure protection across the body?
seoFaqs.answer: Following the standard kavacha-paddhati, each verse appoints a specific Vishnu name to guard a specific anga. Examples include Vishnu protecting the head, Janardana the face, Govinda the throat, Madhava the chest, Hrishikesha the navel, Vamana the thighs, Trivikrama the knees, Sridhara the feet and Achyuta the entire body. Subsequent verses cover the eight directions and the day-night-sandhya cycle, ensuring complete spatial-temporal envelopment. The kavacham concludes with an invocation of the Sudarshana Chakra and the panchayudha (five weapons of Vishnu) as personified protective deities. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to recite Vishnu Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: Thursday (Guruvar) is the traditional weekday for Vishnu and is the most common day for kavacham recitation. Sunday and the eleventh lunar day (Ekadashi) of both fortnights are equally favoured, with Vaikuntha Ekadashi in Margashirsha being the supreme annual occasion. Other peak times include Vishnu Janmashtami, Rama Navami, the Chaturmasa period from Ashadha Shukla Ekadashi to Kartik Shukla Ekadashi, Damodara month (Kartik) for those following Vaishnava lunar observances, and any Dvadashi following an Ekadashi fast. Daily morning recitation after bath is recommended for ongoing protection. (Sources: Drikpanchang; Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vishnu Kavacham promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti promises liberation from every fear: bhuta, preta, pishacha, sarpa, vyaghra, agni, jala, shastra, vyadhi and akala-mrityu. It specifically protects from black magic and abhichara, planetary afflictions, evil eye, court cases, miscarriage and complications in childbirth. Daily recitation is said to grant longevity, freedom from chronic illness, mental peace, prosperity in family and ultimately Vaikuntha-prapti. The text emphasises that protection is most effective when recited with bhakti and ethical living rather than as a mechanical formula. Householders are encouraged to teach the kavacham to children for protection during travel and education. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Vishnu Kavacham differ from Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Sahasranama, found in the Mahabharata Anushasana Parva, is a 1008-name praise hymn focused on glorifying Vishnu's qualities, deeds and cosmic principles; it is recited primarily for spiritual elevation, sin removal and union with Vishnu's nature. The Vishnu Kavacham is a much shorter armor invocation focused specifically on bodily and directional protection, with each name appointed to guard a specific limb or sphere. Devotees often combine the two — the Sahasranama for daily devotion and the Kavacham as an additional protective recitation, especially during travel, illness or hostile circumstances. (Sources: Stotra Nidhi; Hindupedia; Vedadhara.)

seoFaqs.question: Can Vishnu Kavacham be recited without initiation?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As a Pauranika stotra preserved in publicly accessible scriptures, the Vishnu Kavacham requires no diksha and is open to all devotees regardless of varna, gender or age. Children, elderly, women including during menstruation (mental recitation), and householders may all recite it. The traditional preparations are simple: morning bath, clean clothes, a clean east-facing seat, a Vishnu image or Shaligram before the devotee, a lamp lit with sesame or ghee oil, and a clear devotional intention. Pronunciation should be careful but devotion compensates for occasional errors. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Stotra Nidhi.)
